# design-anything — Any design intent in (text, picture) → execution-ready 3D blueprint out (construction- and 3D-print-verified).
# generated from data/*.yml by scripts/build_readme.py — do not hand-edit

## skills
brief-to-blueprint — Compile a text/image design brief into a parametric blueprint plan with explicit constraints and a target ready-gate. [https://github.com/wjlgatech/design-anything/tree/main/skills/brief-to-blueprint]
print-ready-check — Run the ready gate on an STL and report READY/NOT-READY with per-gate evidence. [https://github.com/wjlgatech/design-anything/tree/main/skills/print-ready-check]
construction-ready-check — Run the construction ready gate on a rooms+openings layout — clearances, habitability, daylight, egress, module grid. [https://github.com/wjlgatech/design-anything/tree/main/skills/construction-ready-check]
blueprint-validate — Validate a blueprint against the enduring-principles checklist (anthropometrics, modular grid, daylight, layers). [https://github.com/wjlgatech/design-anything/tree/main/skills/blueprint-validate]
pattern-library — Retrieve applicable Alexander-style patterns (context/problem/solution) for a brief before generating form. [https://github.com/wjlgatech/design-anything/tree/main/skills/pattern-library]
scene-to-layout — Turn a room photo/scan into a structured layout (walls, openings, furniture) using SpatialLM-class tools. [https://github.com/wjlgatech/design-anything/tree/main/skills/scene-to-layout]
design-research — Run the three-window research method (30 days/30 years/300 years) on any design question, with grounding rules. [https://github.com/wjlgatech/design-anything/tree/main/skills/design-research]

## tools
Microsoft TRELLIS.2 — Open (MIT) 4B image-to-3D flow-matching model; any topology, full PBR, GLB/PLY/OBJ export. [https://github.com/microsoft/TRELLIS.2]
Zoo.dev Text-to-CAD (Zookeeper) — Conversational agent emitting true parametric CAD (STEP/DXF, dimensioned) — blueprints, not meshes; open-source, self-hostable. [https://zoo.dev/research/zookeeper]
Meshy-6 — Market-leader SaaS mesh pipeline with PBR texturing, auto-rig, engine plugins, and multi-color 3D-print export. [https://www.meshy.ai/blog/meshy-6-launch]
Hunyuan3D-2 — Fully open asset generator (weights + training code, PBR); momentum shifting to TRELLIS.2. [https://github.com/Tencent-Hunyuan/Hunyuan3D-2]
blender-mcp — Drive Blender from any LLM via MCP — the natural execution backbone from agent to printable geometry. [https://github.com/ahujasid/blender-mcp]
SpatialLM — LLM for structured indoor modeling — point cloud/scene to walls, doors, and furniture layout as structured output. [https://github.com/manycore-research/SpatialLM]
Autodesk Forma (Building Layout Explorer) — Generative interior-layout variants with solar/carbon analysis, pushing straight into Revit. [https://adsknews.autodesk.com/en/news/building-layout-explorer-in-autodesk-forma/]
Finch3D — AI floor-plan copilot; Forma extension unifying massing, space planning, and Revit BIM. [https://parametric-architecture.com/finch-launches-forma-extension/]
ICON (Titan + Vitruvius) — ~250 printed structures; multi-story robotic printing plus an AI architect aiming at permit-ready designs. [https://www.iconbuild.com/newsroom/icon-unveils-new-construction-technologies-for-lowest-cost-fastest-and-most-sustainable-way-to-build-at-scale]
COBOD — Best-selling construction printers in 35+ countries — the "arm the builder" survival pattern. [https://cobod.com/]
WASP — Earth-material construction printing (TECLA habitat) — the sustainable branch that persisted. [https://www.3dwasp.com/]
Marble (World Labs) — Multimodal world model — text/image/video/layout to editable 3D worlds exporting splats, meshes, or video. [https://www.worldlabs.ai/blog/marble-world-model]
NVIDIA Cosmos 3 + Isaac Lab 3.0 — First fully open "omnimodel" world foundation model plus robot-sim stack on the Newton physics engine. [https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/gtc-2026-virtual-worlds-physical-ai/]
Project Genie (Genie 3) — Real-time navigable worlds from text — an ideation layer, not an engine replacement. [https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/google-deepmind/project-genie/]
Blender — The free 3D DCC that survived bankruptcy and won an Oscar — 30-year survivor, default research render tool. [https://www.blender.org/]
Rhino + Grasshopper — The computational-design environment of architecture; never acquired, outlived every challenger. [https://www.rhino3d.com/]
OpenSCAD — Code-CAD for printable parametric parts — the natural target for LLM text-to-CAD since code is an LLM's native 3D output. [https://openscad.org/]
FreeCAD — The open BRep/parametric CAD; reached 1.0 after 22 years. [https://www.freecad.org/]
COLMAP — Default structure-from-motion pipeline every NeRF/3DGS workflow assumes. [https://colmap.github.io/]
Spacely AI — Photoreal room renders with element-level masking, used by boutique studios. [https://www.spacely.ai/]
Planner 5D AI — CAD-lite 3D floor planner with an AI layout/style module. [https://planner5d.com/use/ai-interior-design]

## papers-and-standards
IFC / ISO 16739 — The open BIM interchange standard (born 1996) — legally mandated for public projects in several countries; the survival-grade architecture output target. [https://www.buildingsmart.org/standards/ifc/]
OpenUSD Core Specification — Pixar's scene-graph format, now an industry consortium spec heading to ISO — the presumptive digital-twin standard. [https://aousd.org/news/core-spec-announcement/]
glTF (ISO/IEC 12113) — The JPEG of 3D — default web/AR delivery format. [https://www.khronos.org/gltf/]
3MF — STL's slow-motion successor for 3D printing, default in Bambu Studio/PrusaSlicer/Cura. [https://3mf.io/]
Topology optimization (SIMP; Bendsoe 1988, Sigmund 2001) — The math inside every generative-design tool; survived the hype cycle — how blueprints get structurally validated. [https://www.topopt.mek.dtu.dk/]
Screened Poisson Surface Reconstruction (Kazhdan 2013) — Still the default mesh-from-points algorithm in MeshLab/CloudCompare/Open3D. [https://www.cs.jhu.edu/~misha/MyPapers/ToG13.pdf]
WaveFunctionCollapse (Gumin 2016) — Constraint-based procedural generation shipping in Townscaper, Bad North, Caves of Qud — the PCG open-source success story. [https://github.com/mxgmn/WaveFunctionCollapse]
libigl / discrete differential geometry — The standard mesh-processing toolkit (SGP Software Award); any repair/remesh step sits on it. [https://libigl.github.io/]
A Pattern Language (Alexander 1977) — Composable context/problem/solution patterns — never out of print in 49 years; pre-specifies the architecture of a blueprint generator. [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/A_Pattern_Language]
Architects' Data (Neufert, 1936-) — ~43 editions of anthropometric clearance tables — the generator's hard-constraint database. [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ernst_Neufert]
NeRF (Mildenhall et al. 2020) — Revolutionized 3D representation; already partially displaced by 3DGS — methods churn, representations persist. [https://www.matthewtancik.com/nerf]
3D Gaussian Splatting (Kerbl et al. 2023) — The production real-time capture representation (Polycam, Luma, Scaniverse). [https://repo-sam.inria.fr/fungraph/3d-gaussian-splatting/]
DreamFusion / Score Distillation Sampling (Poole et al. 2022) — The mechanism behind most text-to-3D; now challenged by native 3D generators — may be the first of its batch to die. [https://dreamfusion3d.github.io/]
Instant-NGP (Mueller et al. 2022) — SIGGRAPH 2022 Best Paper; the hash encoding baked into most neural-field trainers. [https://nvlabs.github.io/instant-ngp/]
Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (Ho et al. 2020) — The text-to-image half of the pipeline; Stable Diffusion and successors descend from it. [https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.11239]

## community
Gramazio Kohler Research (ETH Zurich) — Invented "digital fabrication in architecture" as a discipline (2005); alumni seeded labs worldwide. [https://gramaziokohler.arch.ethz.ch/]
Block Research Group (ETH Zurich) — Philippe Block — compression-only structures, 3D-printed floors, and the open-source COMPAS framework. [https://block.arch.ethz.ch/]
Ole Sigmund (DTU TopOpt) — Topology optimization's living anchor; the group's free codes are the field's curriculum. [https://www.topopt.mek.dtu.dk/]
NVIDIA Research — Instant-NGP, Kaolin, Omniverse/OpenUSD, Warp — the graphics/simulation engine room. [https://research.nvidia.com/]
IAAC Barcelona — Academic pipeline for computational and 3D-printed architecture (Open Thesis Fabrication, 3D-printed bridge). [https://iaac.net/]
Bartlett UCL (B-Pro) — One of the three main academic talent pipelines for computational design. [https://www.ucl.ac.uk/bartlett/]
TU Delft — Built-environment research at scale — the third leg of the computational-architecture academy. [https://www.tudelft.nl/en/architecture-and-the-built-environment]
Behrokh Khoshnevis — Contour Crafting — every gantry-extrusion construction printer descends from his patents. [https://contourcrafting.com/]
Fei-Fei Li (World Labs) — Spatial-intelligence world models (Marble) — the strongest current text-to-world lineage. [https://www.worldlabs.ai/]
Maxim Gumin — WaveFunctionCollapse — one person's algorithm shipping in a generation of games. [https://github.com/mxgmn]
Shigeru Miyamoto — Still shipping after 45 years — the deep-time control for game design craft. [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shigeru_Miyamoto]
Will Wright — SimCity/The Sims — his systems survived better than his career; an honest split. [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Will_Wright_(game_designer)]
Pat Hanrahan & Ed Catmull — 2019 Turing Award for fundamental contributions to 3D computer graphics — the certified foundation of this whole pipeline. [https://amturing.acm.org/]
Christopher Alexander (1936-2022) — A Pattern Language — the design book that already survived one substrate change (buildings to software). [https://www.patternlanguage.com/]
Jan Gehl — Human-scale metrics that transformed Copenhagen and were exported worldwide. [https://gehlpeople.com/]
Jane Jacobs (1916-2006) — Overturned the urban-renewal paradigm and stayed overturned; her four conditions live in 2020s zoning reform. [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jane_Jacobs]
Neri Oxman (MIT Media Lab lineage / OXMAN) — Material ecology — biology-informed digital fabrication; the Mediated Matter lineage. [https://oxman.com/]
COBOD — The construction-printing company that survived by arming builders instead of being one. [https://cobod.com/]
ICON — ~250 printed structures and an AI-architect roadmap; restructured 2025 and kept building. [https://www.iconbuild.com/]
buildingSMART International — Stewards of IFC — the 30-year-old open standard everything interoperates through. [https://www.buildingsmart.org/]
Alliance for OpenUSD (AOUSD) — Pixar, Apple, Adobe, Autodesk, NVIDIA, Epic aligning the 3D scene-graph standard. [https://aousd.org/]
Keenan Crane (CMU) — Discrete differential geometry — the curriculum every mesh-processing engineer learns from. [https://www.cs.cmu.edu/~kmcrane/]
